effect.after: Against a structure instead of a creature, this attack deals 1 point of Foundation Damage.

description.value: Rot Grubs act as a group of 8–12 on the same initiative. Splitting the swarm requires an area attack. Each member acts on the same initiative.
description.director: Treat the swarm as a unit. Area attacks are effective against the group. If the party uses targeted strikes, the swarm divides and attacks multiple targets.

description.value: Rot Grubs dissolve stone and mortar on contact. Stone-Gnaw deals 1 point of Foundation Damage to structures. 3 points = wall compromised. 5 points = wall collapses (triggers Grafvitnir access).
description.director: Track Foundation Damage per wall section. This is the primary threat from Rot Grubs — they eat through the western wall's foundation while the party is distracted by the Wight-Commander's assault.

era-of-embers.note: Colony swarm (8–12) eating the western wall's foundation from inside. Used narratively in west wall crisis. Foundation Damage is a special environmental mechanic tracked per-area.

biography.value:
Rot Grub — Level 1 Minion

Beast (Rot-corrupted), Tiny

Stamina 1 | Speed 3 (burrow) | Size T | Stability 0

Pale, segmented worms the length of a forearm, bloated with Rot-corruption. They eat stone, earth, and organic matter with equal indifference. Individually pathetic — in a colony of eight to twelve, they can hollow out a wall's foundation in hours. They are drawn by Wyrd-corruption and tunnel toward sources of binding energy.

Attacks

Gnaw (Melee, Agility): +1 vs Agility; 1 damage. Effect: Target is Sickened until end of their next turn (Rot corruption).

Features

Swarm (8–12): Rot Grubs operate in colonies of 8–12. They do not fight individually in meaningful combat — they are encountered as a swarm in the western wall basement. Use minion rules: 1 Stamina each, die to any hit. The danger is their Foundation Damage, not their combat ability.

Foundation Damage: Each round the colony remains active and uninterrupted, it inflicts 1 Foundation Damage to the area it occupies. Track this per-area:

  • 3 Foundation Damage = Compromised. Visible cracking. The structure above begins to show stress fractures. Defenders must evacuate or risk collapse.
  • 5 Foundation Damage = Collapse. The section gives way entirely. The tunnels below are exposed. Anything above falls into the tunnels. The deep passages are now open.

Burrowing: Rot Grubs have burrow speed 3. They move through stone and earth, leaving tunnels behind them. These tunnels are what Grafvitnir follows upward in Beat 26.

Rot Corruption: Rot Grubs radiate a Rot signature detectable by Wyrd-sense (Intuition). Tracing this signature backward reveals the colony's tunneling pattern and can identify which foundation sections will fail next.
biography.director:
TACTICAL GUIDANCE:

  • Rot Grubs are not a combat encounter — they are an environmental hazard that the party addresses through the West Wall crisis track of the Three-Crisis Montage.
  • Individually, a grub dies to any hit. The challenge is locating and clearing the colonies before they accumulate enough Foundation Damage to compromise the wall.
  • The Foundation Damage mechanic represents the escalating structural threat. Each round of inattention costs the party another point of Foundation Damage.
  • Even with 3+ successes on the West Wall crisis, the deep tunnels remain open. The party can seal surface sections but cannot reach the deep passages. This is intentional — Grafvitnir follows these tunnels upward regardless.
  • The question is not whether the tunnels exist but how wide they are when Grafvitnir uses them.
